methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Adarga is a Series B defense and security company that has raised $26.2M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers—all operating in the same sector and stage. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns, among others.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $26.2M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage progression, implying a valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis draws on six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.2B, anchored by valuations ranging from $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns) to $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security). The peer-median method directly implies $1.2B.

The triangulated range spans $42M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $625M. The wide dispersion reflects methodological divergence: stage-progression yields a more conservative figure based on capital raised, while peer-median captures the valuations commanded by comparable-stage defense tech companies. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Adarga's product differentiation, customer traction, or team pedigree merits positioning toward the peer median versus the capital-raised baseline. Additional diligence on revenue, customer concentration, and competitive positioning would narrow this range materially.
